items.title: Circuit Breaker Pattern in Go: Stop Cascading Failures
items.description:
A circuit breaker stops your Go service from hammering a failing dependency,
preventing cascading failures that consume goroutines, sockets, and memory until the entire system collapses.

The hard part is not the state machine. It is deciding where the breaker belongs, what counts as fa

items.title: How to Catch False “Done” Claims in AI-Generated Code
items.description:
AI-generated code can sound finished long before it is ready to accept. A message such as “tests passed” is a claim, not a verdict. Before accepting delivery, ask a more useful question: what evidence actually supports the claim that the requested work is complete?

items.title: translateZ()
items.description: The translateZ() function moves an element closer to or farther from the user. translateZ() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.
